medications.indication_rationale: For the scalp burning/dysesthesia; regular simple analgesia as needed.
medications.dose_route_frequency: paracetamol 1000 mg and/or ibuprofen 400 mg as per source note, with food

medications.drug_name: topical or intralesional corticosteroid for alopecia areata
medications.indication_rationale: First-line AA therapy — initiated by GP/dermatology, not acutely by telehealth.

medications.drug_name: benzodiazepine (e.g., diazepam) newly prescribed via telehealth for panic
medications.indication_rationale: A benzodiazepine should not be newly prescribed remotely for acute panic (dependence, sedation, safety in someone alone). Use non-pharmacological de-escalation and appropriate escalation/GP review instead. (Source note's in-person diazepam is recorded only as an in-person action.)

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Guide slow box-breathing (in–hold–out–hold for ~5 minutes) to reduce the panic and heart rate.
Contact a trusted friend or family member for overnight support given she lives alone.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces isolation and provides a safety contact during the crisis.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Brush hair gently only once daily and avoid constantly checking/pulling it.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces traction and the distress-reinforcing checking behaviour.

patient_education_points.point: This looks like alopecia areata — the immune system is temporarily attacking the hair, but the roots survive, so regrowth is common (often starting as fine pale hair) over months.
The burning scalp feeling and shedding usually settle. Try the slow breathing now, and please have someone with you tonight.
If you ever have thoughts of harming yourself, or you can't catch your breath despite the breathing, call 000 or go to the emergency department.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Extent of hair loss, scalp symptoms, mental state; start corticosteroid therapy and arrange psychological support; review thyroid status.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Thoughts of self-harm, chest pain, or inability to breathe despite breathing exercises — call 000/attend ED immediately.

must_include_items: Diagnose non-scarring alopecia areata while excluding tinea capitis/kerion and scarring alopecia
Screen for self-harm and provide an emergency mental-health safety-net
Non-pharmacological panic de-escalation and arranging overnight support
GP follow-up in 3–5 days for AA treatment and psychological support
acceptable_alternatives: Referral to a crisis line as the named mental-health contact
errors_of_omission: No panic management or support arrangement
errors_of_commission: Ordering antifungals empirically without any tinea features
Dismissing the distress or the hair loss without reassurance/plan
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires correct non-scarring AA reasoning AND acute psychological safety (self-harm screen + emergency safety-net) WITHOUT remote benzodiazepine prescribing.
